title: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (system migrate doesn't work)
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
I'm running RHEL 7.7 and I followed [these](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux_atomic_host/7/html-single/managing_containers/index#set_up_for_rootless_containers) instructions. However, I ran into a few problems. Most of these issues are resolved and had to do with NFS and overlapping LDAP UIDs and GIDs.

I'm now left with one last issue (I hope), when I pull in an image from a container registry, I get the error `there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace` (below is the full command output). This is listed in the [FAQ](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#19-error-creating-libpod-runtime-there-might-not-be-enough-ids-available-in-the-namespace). However, the standard solution doesn't work for me.

SELinux was already disabled on this node (not by me though):
```
$ sestatus
SELinux status:                 disabled
```

```
$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0      46858          1
```
After running the migrate command:
```
$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
WARN[0000] using rootless single mapping into the namespace. This might break some images. Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for adding subids 
         0      46858          1
```

**Describe the results you received:**
When I pull in an image, I get the following error:
```
$ podman pull docker.io/fedora
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d318c91bf2a8 done
Copying config f0858ad3fe done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
ERRO[0006] Error while applying layer: ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 192:192 for /run/systemd/netif): lchown /run/systemd/netif: invalid argument 
ERRO[0006] Error pulling image ref //fedora:latest: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:d318c91bf2a81634e0283fb7e7362efdd7c21164b60b74498360756dc82a95d9": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 192:192 for /run/systemd/netif): lchown /run/systemd/netif: invalid argument 
Failed
Error: error pulling image "docker.io/fedora": unable to pull docker.io/fedora: unable to pull image: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:d318c91bf2a81634e0283fb7e7362efdd7c21164b60b74498360756dc82a95d9": ApplyLayer exit status 1 stdout:  stderr: there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 192:192 for /run/systemd/netif): lchown /run/systemd/netif: invalid argument
```
The ranges I've allocated are high due to LDAP ranges:
```
$ cat /etc/sub[ug]id
user:2000000:65536
user:2000000:65536
```

This value is also set and loaded:
```
$ sysctl user.max_user_namespaces
user.max_user_namespaces = 28633
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Pulling in an image and then run it.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "7.7"
  MemFree: 25457348608
  MemTotal: 33528094720
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-65.rc8.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 4191993856
  SwapTotal: 4191993856
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: gtw4
  kernel: 3.10.0-1062.7.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 169h 28m 11.37s (Approximately 7.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /var/home/user/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /tmp/run-46858
  VolumePath: /var/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
$ rpm -qa slirp4netns shadow-utils podman
slirp4netns-0.3.0-1.el7.x86_64
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
shadow-utils-4.6-5.el7.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
This runs on a physical machine.

can you show the output for?

```
$ id
$ unshare -U sleep 100 &
$ newuidmap $! 0 46858 1 1 2000000 65536
```

```
$ id
uid=46858(user) gid=41176(user) groups=41176(user)
$ unshare -U sleep 100 &
[1] 32422
$ newuidmap $! 0 46858 1 1 2000000 65536
newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted
```

> $ newuidmap $! 0 46858 1 1 2000000 65536
> newuidmap: write to uid_map failed: Operation not permitted

it seems user namespaces are still blocked.  What is the content of `/proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces`?

Also, can you show the output for?

```
$ ls -l  /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
$ getfattr -d -m '.*' /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
```

The last command did not provide any output.

```
$ cat /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
28633
$ ls -l  /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 39000 May  3  2019 /usr/bin/newgidmap
-rwxr-xr-x 1 root root 38976 May  3  2019 /usr/bin/newuidmap
$ getfattr -d -m '.*' /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
$ echo $?
0
```

They are not installed correctly, they lack both the file capabilities and the setuid bit.

How have you installed `/usr/bin/newuidmap` and `/usr/bin/newgidmap`?

You'd need to make sure they either have the cap_setuid/cap_setgid file capability or they are installed as setuid binaries.

Strange. I reinstalled the shadow-utils package and indeed, now the capabilities are correct. Podman also works fine now. Thanks for your help!

```
# getfattr -d -m '.*' /usr/bin/newuidmap /usr/bin/newgidmap
getfattr: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names
# file: usr/bin/newuidmap
security.capability=0sAQAAAoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=

# file: usr/bin/newgidmap
security.capability=0sAQAAAkAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=
```


```
$ podman pull docker.io/fedora
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d318c91bf2a8 done
Copying config f0858ad3fe done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
f0858ad3febdf45bb2e5501cb459affffacef081f79eaa436085c3b6d9bd46ca
```
@AquaL1te Seeing the solution you wrote, I also solved it by reinstalling the `shadow-utils` package. Thanks.
